title: Kouru Dummy System
wikitext:
[]: Kouru Dummy System             Quote Post by Isanov » Sat Dec 24, 2005 12:21 pm Hypothesis: The Dummy Plugs used in the Eva Series had humans inside, and its most likely they were Shinji's classmates. Known: The Dummy Plug system is a mental imprint of a person (Kouru) but requires an eva child to operate (as shown in ep#14). Now many Eva Children were located in Shinji's classmates, but its likely there were more arround the world and at the other Nerv HQs. The Eva Series all had artificial S2 engines and were different than Eva 00-04, but they should still need a eva child to operate (else why have dummy "plugs"). We did not see any of Shinji's classmates die in 3rd impact. Conclusion: There is nothing I can think of to contradict this theory. Thoughts: I admit its a sick, twisted thing, but it fits the series and the overall tone of EoE. It would also make for a great shot (Hikari weeping as she heard Asuka dying) but would take too much time to explain/cause confusion in the movie.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Dec 24, 2005 12:27 pm I can't vote in the pole it won't let me. Some one call the monkey.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Dec 24, 2005 1:21 pm This is a novel suggestion, and would seem to have some backing, in the omitted sequence from EoE in which Touji and Kensuke talk with Shinji about their being evacuated to a NERV facility in Germany. It would give a Keel-like reason for that particular move. And it makes a chance from the usual hypothesis of a Kaworu-quarium from which clones were decanted into the harpies' dummy plugs. However episode 17 suggests that the system is entirely mechanical (well, all electronic): [code:1]Ritsuko:This is the prototype dummy plug. Rei's personality was transfered, although the human mind and spirit are not digitalizable. This is nothing but a fake, a copy which imitates the pilot's thinking. It's nothing but a machine. Gendou: It transmits a signal pattern to Eva. Eva considers a pilot to be in it and synchronizes with this. That's what we need. Install the data to Unit One, and Two. Ritsuko: It still has some problems. Gendou: I don't care. If Eva moves, that will be fine. Ritsuko: Understood.[/code:1] where the harpies are presumably being directed by something which bears the same relationship to Kaworu as the MAGI do to Naoko.

[]: Re: Kouru Dummy System             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Sat Dec 24, 2005 3:45 pm Isanov wrote: Known: The Dummy Plug system is a mental imprint of a person (Kouru) but requires an eva child to operate (as shown in ep#14).

[]:             Quote Post by Isanov » Sat Dec 24, 2005 5:41 pm Ahh well, it was a nice supposition. I took gendo-ritsuko's talk to mean the dummy plug was a partial failure, that they could not get it to operate independent of a pilot (thus why Shinji and Asuka were not sent home). What is the official material you mention AA?

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Sat Dec 24, 2005 5:53 pm The Death and Rebirth theatrical program, deluxe edition. Check it out at evaotaku.com.

[]:             Quote Post by Rhine » Sat Dec 24, 2005 6:09 pm Um, if you look at the entry plugs of the MP Eva Series while they're airborn and before they "plug in" as it were, you see KAWORU written on the plugs. While it's a neat theory that Class 2-A were sent off to Germany for Eva piloting, it's not substantiated by anything actually in the films.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Dec 24, 2005 7:06 pm Rhine wrote: Um, if you look at the entry plugs of the MP Eva Series while they're airborn and before they "plug in" as it were, you see KAWORU written on the plugs. While it's a neat theory that Class 2-A were sent off to Germany for Eva piloting, it's not substantiated by anything actually in the films.

[]:             Quote Post by ThroneofDravaris » Sun Dec 25, 2005 8:25 am I always thought that all of the MPEs shared Kaworuâ€™s soul (aka Adam), which explains how he became a part of Lilith when the MPAs assimilated with her. Unless of course Kaworuâ€™s soul returned to Adamâ€™s body, which now that I think about it is probably equally as likelyâ€¦ Hmmâ€¦ EDIT: It would seem that once again I have posted something that had absolutely no relevance to the topic. Just Ignore all that then...

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Dec 26, 2005 12:39 pm The fate of Class 2A is quite the mystery, given as Mr. Tines mentioned above, they most likely ended up in Germany; the lair of the beast (SEELE) so to speak. A gruesome fate entombed in eithe rthe MP Evas or the dummy plugs certainly gives a perversly attractive additon to the theatre of "Grand Guignol" that is End Of Evangelion. Yet it does not fit very well, and is nowhere supported by any hint or indication, and is indeed contradicted by the nature of the Rei dummy system. However, in episode #25 and #26, Shinji is joined by select classmates? Or is it simply comlpementation playing tricks on us again?

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon Dec 26, 2005 5:55 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: The fate of Class 2A is quite the mystery, given as Mr. Tines mentioned above, they most likely ended up in Germany; the lair of the beast (SEELE) so to speak. A gruesome fate entombed in eithe rthe MP Evas or the dummy plugs certainly gives a perversly attractive additon to the theatre of "Grand Guignol" that is End Of Evangelion. Yet it does not fit very well, and is nowhere supported by any hint or indication, and is indeed contradicted by the nature of the Rei dummy system. However, in episode #25 and #26, Shinji is joined by select classmates? Or is it simply comlpementation playing tricks on us again?

[]:             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Tue Dec 27, 2005 12:36 am I always thought it was a pretty simple thing. The evas dont need a person inside aslong as they got the dummy system. and not to mention that all of Shinji's classmates (besides Rei and Asuka) are gone to other parts of the world cus T3 is well... poof! I doubt Seele would be able to catch them, and why would they at that.

[]:             Quote Post by Isanov » Thu Dec 29, 2005 3:35 am TheBlueTree wrote: I always thought it was a pretty simple thing. The evas dont need a person inside aslong as they got the dummy system. and not to mention that all of Shinji's classmates (besides Rei and Asuka) are gone to other parts of the world cus T3 is well... poof! I doubt Seele would be able to catch them, and why would they at that.

[]:             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Sat Dec 31, 2005 12:47 am My one question (at the moment theres tons more to bring up later :P) is why do they even need the dummy plug system??? They really only need to kill the other angels so they wouldnt start 3I. But did they really need the eva series to start the 3I useing unit 1. I mean the Seele members say in EoE that they no longer have the Lance of Longus so they cannot complete the...(blah blah blah u know the story) so they have to use liliths offspring (blah blah unit 1 blah blah). Anyway my question would be... did they really need the eva series to create 3I or was it just a the situation that they needed them?

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Dec 31, 2005 12:58 am Ok you should know that the answers you want arn't given clearly in the series. To the best of my information its implyed that whoever starts 3I will control it. The EVA series exsisted to start 3I and seeing as they were controlled by Seele, it would give Keel control of 3I. The dumy plugs let Seele/Keel control the eva series with out a pilot.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sat Dec 31, 2005 1:03 pm Does anyone have any idea exactly how one even gives commands to the dummy plug?

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Dec 31, 2005 2:17 pm SEELE 08 wrote: Does anyone have any idea exactly how one even gives commands to the dummy plug?

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sat Dec 31, 2005 5:10 pm Its probably a PDA app for all we know. :)

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sun Jan 01, 2006 2:15 am The real reason the eva series was so savage is Keel got the blue screen of death :)

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sun Jan 01, 2006 11:35 am I started thinking (which is a dangerous thing for me to do) maybe Keel can control via some of his cybernetics?
